body:
HIGH, erasure-integrity (H7/H9). Found by the loam-erasure lens during T67's P5 (2026-07-23). RE-SCOPED 2026-07-24 after T67 landed: part (a)'s SURFACING is already done — cli.ts (the boot heal) now loops `healed.purgeFailures` and prints a loud stderr warning per refusal, with the H9 rationale (cli.ts ~146-152). What T67 did NOT do, and this ticket now covers:

(b) HEAL'S OWN BYTE VERDICT. `heal` still trusts `purge`'s return: a sweep that returns a count and does not throw is taken as done. That is the exact §11 conflation T40 caught elsewhere — a purge can report success while bytes sit at rest (a freelist page, a .tmp straggler, a WAL image the count never saw). `erase` was converted to a `holds`-based verdict (survivingTombstones); heal was not. FIX: after the sweep, ask each tier `holds(id)` for every dead id and record any survivors in HealReport (e.g. `survivingPrimary`/`survivingMirror`, ids that a purge claimed yet a tier still holds). Counts stay as EVIDENCE OF WORK; the holds answer is the VERDICT.

(a-remainder) DEGRADED LATCH. Surfacing to stderr is necessary but ephemeral. A refused-or-incomplete sweep should LATCH a durable degraded signal rather than let serve continue as if clean — the store keeps serving (refusing to boot trades a leak for an outage, per the existing comment), but the degraded state is observable (a flag on the report the boot path acts on; do not silently swallow). Keep it a REPAIR: surface + latch, do not refuse boot.

RAILS (P3). (1) Drive a tier whose `purge` REFUSES (throws) through heal and assert `purgeFailures` names it AND the boot path tells the operator (object level: assert what `serve`/io.err actually writes, not just the struct) — the catch branch at mirror.ts is entered by NO existing test today (`purgeFailures` appears only as `[]`). (2) Drive a tier whose `purge` RETURNS SUCCESS but leaves the id `holds`-true, and assert heal's new verdict reports the survivor — object level, not the count. Assert at BOTH levels (delta/count AND holds/serve output) per the P3 discipline.

Scope: src/store/mirror.ts (heal + HealReport), src/cli/cli.ts (boot heal reads the verdict + latch), test/store/mirror.test.ts. Evidence: T67 P5 loam-erasure finding; SUBSTRATE-HAZARDS H7/H9.

--- BUILD STATUS 2026-07-24 (branch t70-heal-byte-verdict, NOT merged) ---
Part (b) BUILT: heal now runs a verify pass — after the sweeps it asks each tier holds(id) for every dead id and routes any survivor (or a tier that cannot answer, per H9) into purgeFailures (no HealReport shape change, so T67's frozen mirror.test.ts is untouched). Rails in test/store/heal-byte-verdict.test.ts, P3/P4 green, hollow-test: the changed heal code has zero surviving mutants.
P5 (3 independent lenses, routed by npm run p5): loam-erasure CLEAN (§11/H7 completeness, H9, order/re-add all confirmed correct); loam-hollow-rail CLEAN (rails non-hollow, object-level). loam-scan-scale ONE CONFIRMED finding: the verify pass calls holds per dead id, and ArchiveBackend.holds pays a FULL archive sweep on a negative answer, so the healthy boot path is O(dead x archive) — a latency cliff (H8) on the per-boot heal path, growing with tombstone count x archive size, on the SUCCESS path. Same shape already inverted for ArchiveBackend.purge (T55). So T70 does NOT self-merge (clean-audit bar fails).

REMAINING (needs Myk / a deliberate lap): the single-pass inversion. Add an OPTIONAL seam method (e.g. ) that a tier answers in ONE pass; ArchiveBackend implements it as a single sweep building a present-set (canonical + straggler names) intersected with the dead set (O(archive+dead)); heal's verify prefers it and falls back to per-id holds for tiers without it (memory/sqlite holds are already cheap). This changes the STORE CONTRACT (new optional method), so it leans decide-not-repair — Myk's call on the seam shape. Rail it at the ARCHIVE-DRIVER level too (hollow-rail noted the current rails prove the verdict against a simulated combinator-level lie, not a real freelist/WAL/.tmp straggler). AT LANDING: also fix SUBSTRATE-HAZARDS.md:253 — its T70 note still says 'nothing in src/ reads purgeFailures', but cli.ts:146 now does (T67 landed that surfacing).
title: The boot sweep can fail in total silence — heal's purgeFailures has no reader
